author: @willhibbardii2450
text: My 2 cents... Facing hopelessness and overcoming it. Hope is a choice! It's a series and parallels of adjustments to comprehend. When I'm charged to engineer a complex system from cradle to manifest... Often information and incomplete pieces are missing to see everything. A tool, that I employ is logging a 5-minute increment logbook. I make a decision in this process every 5 minutes. It creates the flow and timing of locating the needed information. It also indicates unfamiliar awareness and how much homework study. Overtime the 5-minute log becomes very simple and unnecessary. I learned to do this navigating my aircraft. I take the map and plan the route from departure to arrival. If a storm is in my path, I have a choice of landing before it gets rough, performing a 180 and retreat, going around it, flying over or under it or going through it. In my youth I decided intentionally to fly through a storm. I didn't like it however it taught me a lesson on exactly how rough, rough can be. I can't control the weather and since then I have been caught in un-forecasted rapid developing bad weather that has caused me to land at an alternate unintended destination. During these times of finding myself in unfamiliar surroundings plans change. I've learned when I'm grounded such as today that taking time to see what's going on and explore. Reading the comments and writing this after reading them has been a pleasurable experience and diversion. Thank you for posting this, Robert. This was helpful to me as a mini vacation. It was a good choice to take time out to watch and respond. Stay positive my friends and have a blessed day.

author: @jameshughes3014
text:
Those are all timeless, great tips and as a person who struggle with depression I know they work.
The best thing for me is to just build something. When life seems hopeless and everything seems out of control, if i can build a little doodad or whatchamacallit it gives me a sense of agency and then my problems don't seem so big. It might be a gadget, or a piece of art, or a bit of music. Just making anything at all, the act of making something exist, there's something very therapeutic about it.

Agreed! to believe in Hopelessness though one needs to believe in the concept of hope wich includes believing in future as if it's as real as only the very moment can be.
And "after all" the issue is not hope or the lack of, but identifying with it.
trying to find the answer to "who am i substantially?" in finding "stuff" to identify with is - as far as i'm aware :) - a sickness most of us suffer from and the cause of pretty much any discord from the quarrel up to war. 
identifying with mental constructs - it even sounds kinda, let's say: reduced :)

author: @OldManSparkplug
text:
There are really two things I watch this channel for.

The first is practical demonstrations of technology in plain English, the second is is for your presentation of those demonstrations.

I truly appreciate seeing a person who fundamentally *understands* the the underpinnings of an idea getting excited by it.  That excitement is contagious.  I'd largely switched from building proof-of-concept things to dust-collecting knick-knacks before finding this channel.  Now I'm much happier to be back tinkering with bits and bobs of things.

author: @KimberlyRPeacock
text: Thank you Robert.  That spoke to me.  I am so much better at understanding things that I sometimes get lost in the things as contrasted to understanding why we care about things in the first place.  Since Deb had the brain injury I have just been spiraling out of control.  I would have traded places with her if I could have.  I islanded myself away from the world, because I was embarrassed how I let myself go, and then because I did not keep in touch so now how do I ask for help.   I’m getting a bit more positive about the future. I have as many obstacles as ever.  But like the Phoenix I rise.

replies.author: @TnTtalktime

replies.author: @KimberlyRPeacock
replies.text: @TnTtalktime My desktop computer is down right now as when I had an electrical fire a couple months ago it fried a couple of components. I am working off from my iPad right now.  So am using Onshape for cad as it’s free but any files you create are public.  I am in process of learning, but most cad systems have similar ways of doing things.  I’m going to build a cad model of this rubberized hydride stirling engine.  Two types. One where the output will be as a hydraulic pump which we can use to drive a motor attached to a generator, and another which outputs pulsatile dc power rectifies it and converts to AC.  I’m going to restrict polymers to above 10C and below 150C.  The reason is that for residential cogeneration we can use ground water as lower bound and a heat pump to push temp to 100 C or just below. Heating and cooling is primary energy use in residences and water can be stored in tanks and act as cheap thermal batteries and the lower the temp differential between use temp and storage temp the less losses we shall have in thermal storage.  I cant do full fluid and thermal analysis with my desktop down,  but I can use Mathematica cloud and some FEA and try my hand at hand coding the system modellica embedded in Mathematica now.  No promises on the systems model, but should have a cad model in a day or two and will share with all.  Same goes for the physics model but that will take bit longer.  The goal is 1 KW or better. My thinking is on average most residences average 1 to 2 KW/hr for the month.  The downside to rubber and cross linked polymers is low thermal conductivity, but look at what MIT was able to do with epdm.   Hydrides are also known for their low thermal conductivity but 1 mol of hydrogen can yield 22 KJ of heating or cooling and pressure can double and half within a 15-20 C delta T.  The rubber may actually help keep thermal energy in the system once injected.  We can even encase with oxygen free copper to minimize hydrogen diffusion and loss.  My thinking is low cost light weight and theoretically up to a watt per gram with these elastomers,  it’s worth a go at it.

author: @davidl.howser9707
text: Robert, I'm stil using my mobile "smarter than me"  Samsung Note-8 phone just for the same reason you commented about delaying a purchase. My Scottish GrandFather's advice to me when considering a purchase was to....." postpone the buying date" , and by looking inside yourself for soothing contentment. Why not base purchases on need, versus want was for me a lesson that stuck. Often want is a driver to have a flourish of quick to wear off joy brought by a new purchase like sugary sweets leaving a person very soon to be looking once again for your next high from another, and another from the next new purchase based on a fleeting joy from want. Better is to look inside to find out why a person wants material objects to find the good feeling of internal joy where little joy exists. Hope this writing helps others take a moment to evaluate their steering hands action on the rudder churning through life's sometimes turbulent currents to a better place of still waters knowing all your needs are met.

author: @calvingreene90
text: Who is our current technology the Earth can support 200 billion people.

replies.author: @Renew_You
replies.text:
Probably Forest Gump! "Life is like a box of chocolates!" I think life is like a bunch of Jalapenos... What you do today will burn your butt tomorrow! 🤣🤣🤣 I asked Bing AI, and this is what it said, I'm sorry, I couldn't find any information about a writer who claimed that the Earth can support 200 billion people. According to the majority of studies, the Earth's capacity is at or beneath 8 billion people. However, the actual human carrying capacity of Earth is a topic of debate among scientists and estimates range from 500 million people to more than one trillion. It's important to note that the carrying capacity of Earth is not just a matter of quantity, but also quality. Even if Earth could theoretically support over one trillion people, the quality of life for those people would be a concern.
If you have any other questions, please let me know. 😊In my current world in the DFW region of the US we're experiencing an invasion from all over the world approaching 9 million population without the infrastructure to support that many. Traffic, water, energy, jobs, housing, medical, trash, recycle, local politics, inflation, food, trucking but business is booming... However, the locals are feeling displaced and find us thinking and feeling, oh no! Fallen angels have left their home and bringing the reasons they left here.

author: @stephen1475
text:
Hopelessness can be considered a form of self loathing  combined with sadness resulting in what is effectively self remorse. According to plutchicks model of emotion, the oposite is love. Hopelessness also isnt far away from loneliness.
The trick is to realise that sometimes we just cant love ourself. So ask someone to help you out of your hole. Start with a hug. Youll be surprised how much it can help.

author: @scotttovey
text:
The only real reason that things are hopeless in some people's minds is because they are simply lazy.

They want to take a mountain of a problem, pick up a garden shovel, and move that mountain out of the way with a single shovel full. 

They don't want to make it a life priority to keep shoveling, moving little by little until the mountain has been moved and the problem has been solved. 

That however, is the only way to solve the big problems, little by little, precept upon precept. Here a little there a little.

Did you know that there is a verse in the Bible that describes 3D additive printing? No, it's not prophesying 3D printing technology, but it is very interesting that Isaiah describes the word of the Lord in the terms of 3D printing.

Isaiah 28:10: For precept must be upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little:

author: @guygustard3340
text:
Carer 23-yrs; 65-yrs old. Daughter 19, Necrotic Frontal Lobe, has no friends, I'm her sally-anne, her confessor, her only friend. She is positive. I am exhausted after all these years. She's positive as I gave everything I had.

She will survive my death, I won't.

My hopelessness is a product of watching her rejected daily. My constant meetings for her support. The constant ignorance we have heaped on us about her.

My solution was to start a Carers Group. Began in May, 40 members, no interaction because we're all Carers with no time. So my last ditch positive effort was a flop.... Next year, there's always next year, but it sucks.

author: @technosaurus3805
text:
My 15 year old son was a big fan of this channel before he was murdered just over a year ago. He was so hopeful for his future because he KNEW all of the existential problems were solvable because, thanks to Robert (and others) he was able to invent many solutions himself despite having disgraphia and ADD.
Robert's battery series was the inspiration for his experimental fuel cell+supercapacitor+NiFe battery.
When he realized our current  grid wouldn't support all electric vehicles, he came up with the idea to use liquid nitrogen and ambient temperature as the "fuel" for air-motor powered vehicles (much faster "recharges" too)
When he learned about overpopulation and world hunger he decided that recycling plastics into ocean floating gardens and greenhouses with salt tolerant plants like the Nypa palm above and other marine species below could get us to a 10 trillion human capacity (but would require more CO2, not less).
There was a bunch of silly kid inventions along the way too but it brings me some hope that future generations night continue to be inspired by channels like this.

replies.author: @Pasandeeros
replies.text:
@TnTtalktime Do you mean that hope which escaped from the Pandora's box?

There is actually not even need for hope, except perhaps if you''re playing lottery.😆

Everything is just probabilities and if you're knowledgeable enough there is simply no room for hope. It's dumb to "hope" for the main prize in a lottery since you know exactly what the probability of winning is.

In a small scale one can change those probabilities by taking action to favor the future they'd like to see happen.

In a larger (Human System) scale the future is pretty much fixed already to certain general outcome due to the immense size, inertia and stiffness of the system and the biophysical limits.
